title: ED asthma exacerbation documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.
assessment.rationale: The exact DHA asthma symptoms section supports documenting onset, severity, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and clinician-assessed risk context for a suspected exacerbation without inferring severity class or diagnosis.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports wheeze, cough, breathlessness, chest tightness, variation, nocturnal or seasonal pattern, triggers, exercise and occupational context, atopy or family history, bronchodilator response, respiratory examination, peak flow, and objective confirmation.

assessment.text: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.
assessment.rationale: The exact red-flag section supports recording measured vital data, general appearance and time-critical symptoms only when assessed.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports sudden or severe breathing difficulty, cardiac or chest symptoms, cyanosis, inability to speak full sentences, confusion or agitation, stridor or audible wheeze, immobility or trauma, orthopnoea, status asthmaticus, and near-fatal asthma history as clinician-assessed prompts.

assessment.text: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.
assessment.rationale: The exact investigations section supports documenting existing tests reviewed by the clinician without recommending a test.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports peak flow, pulmonary function testing, spirometry, bronchodilator reversibility, and selected clinician-led investigations.

assessment.text: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.
assessment.rationale: The exact review and referral section supports recording escalation, specialist discussion or follow-up only when already decided or arranged by the clinician.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Asthma – 38
source.population: People presenting with suspected or established asthma in the populations specified by the guideline.
source.setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
source.exclusions: Direct for Dubai telehealth asthma documentation; emergency management and controlled-medicine prescribing are excluded and in-person use remains partial.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports review of alternative or comorbid causes, adherence, inhaler technique, smoking, occupational, psychosocial and environmental factors, treatment response, device review, and clinician-led referral.

assessment.text: Defines DHA telehealth scope, licensed-facility applicability, and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.
assessment.rationale: Virtual-care scope and applicability were reviewed; no automatic in-person examination or emergency treatment was inferred.
